reason: While adding redundant DNS servers is a standard hardening practice, modifying /etc/resolv.conf can lead to immediate loss of network connectivity or name resolution if incorrect IP addresses are provided. In modern RHEL 9 systems, /etc/resolv.conf is often managed by NetworkManager; manual edits may be overwritten or cause conflicts with existing network configurations.
estimated_impact: networking affected; Incorrect DNS IP addresses can break name resolution; Manual edits may be overwritten by NetworkManager; Potential loss of connectivity to remote services/repositories
